source: CHANGELOG.md and immutable published Dev source commits

1.0.0.title: First Stable release
1.0.0.summary: The production Cloth NeXt Bake workflow, with multi-object simulation, documented diagnostics and cumulative update channels.

sections.label: Added
entries: Multi-object Cloth, Rod / Cable and Soft Body Bakes, including Follow Animation Pins and colliderless projects.
Keyframeable Empty Forces for every PPF force parameter exposed by Cloth NeXt.
Seventy-four stable CNX error codes with persistent local diagnostics and atomic per-run failure reports.

sections.label: Changed
entries: Stable publishes to Stable, Beta and Dev; Beta publishes to Beta and Dev; Dev remains Dev-only.
Companion transport failures remain visible, pulse red and require explicit acknowledgement.
Authenticated Companion status messages are bounded and tolerate unknown future enum values.

sections.label: Fixed
entries: RAM safety cancellation now terminates as actionable CNX-E166.
Worker tracebacks and complete Bake failures persist without masking the original solver error.
Channel validation accepts more-stable candidates while rejecting less-stable or ambiguous indices.

1.0.5.title: Animated preparation reliability
1.0.5.summary: A focused Dev snapshot for heavy animated-collider preparation and continuously updating resource telemetry.

sections.label: Changed
entries: Animated Collider capture publishes frame-level progress instead of flooding the Companion with every motion sub-sample.
The Companion drains buffered preparation status in bounded batches and keeps only the latest visual update.
System Load graphs refresh on the telemetry timer even when the Blender viewport is idle.

sections.label: Fixed
entries: Bake readiness commands can no longer sit behind a large animated-capture status backlog.
Non-blocking Companion socket reads no longer surface BlockingIOError as a transport failure.

sections.label: Validation
entries: Source imports, Companion build and hash, Blender extension validation, and forbidden-artifact scans passed.
Real PPF integration, interactive Blender acceptance, and full release preflight were not part of this Dev snapshot.
